mapping.a_element: the genetic algorithm's evolutionary search over a real-valued genome (mutation/selection over generations)
mapping.b_element: the biological/cultural evolutionary process that generated protein and melody sequences

mapping.a_element: the 14,602-gene real-valued genome encoding the network
mapping.b_element: the one-dimensional protein/melodic sequences drawn from a limited alphabet

brief_justification: The overlap is only the shared vocabulary of 'evolution'/'genome': paper A's machinery is genetic-algorithm optimization (fitness-driven search over a genome) that designs a delay-mediated neural classifier, whereas paper B's machinery is bioinformatics sequence analysis (rhythm-aware alignment, substitution matrices, positional conservation, covariance) that statistically characterizes already-evolved sequences. A runs an evolutionary optimization; B does not optimize anything but instead aligns and analyzes sequences, so no role-for-role mapping of the driving process holds.
